questions.assertion: A trader prepares a complete record of purchases and sales. He is required to prepare a Trading Account.
questions.reason: Since the purchases and sales are fully recorded, the profits and losses can be ascertained in the Trading Account.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The statement is true because the trader prepares a Trading Account, and the reason is also correct because complete records of purchases and sales enable the determination of profits and losses.

questions.assertion: In case of incomplete records, the trader is not required to prepare a Trading Account.
questions.reason: Trading Account is prepared only when the complete records of purchases and sales are available.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The statement is true because the trader is not required to prepare a Trading Account in case of incomplete records, and the reason is also correct because Trading Account requires complete records of purchases and sales.
